headline: The best of FT Alphaville, 2025 edition
summary: TL;DR: lots and lots of navel-gazing
body_text:
Whatever your personal views, we can all agree that it’s been a year. For Alphaville, 2025 involved strawberry sandwiches, Sam Altman’s olive oil habits, Michael Saylor and lots and lots and lots of charts.

In no order whatsoever — and possibly subject to some recency bias — here are some of our own greatest hits from the past twelve months, plus a few absolute bombs that we wanted to resurface out of sheer obstinacy.

A reminder: everything below is free to read, you just need to register with an email. A second reminder: we also have a newsletter, which is also free.

Oracle is already underwater on its ‘astonishing’ $300bn OpenAI deal

Bryce said that if his shitpost on Oracle and the “Curse of OpenAI” became better read than mainFT’s epic The Line article it would signal the death of journalism. Well, RIP journalism.

Valve conquered PC gaming. What comes next?

We love exploring “black hole” companies — ultra-private businesses that must largely be observed by the influence they
